description: Flask before 1.0 is vulnerable to an issue where unexpected memory usage can occur when handling unparsed JSON, leading to a Denial of Service (DoS).
Flask version 0.12.2 and earlier contains a Resource injection vulnerability in the application logger that can result in log injection.

Flask is a lightweight WSGI web application framework. When all of the following conditions are met, a response containing data intended for one client may be cached and subsequently sent by the proxy to other clients. If the proxy also caches `Set-Cookie` headers, it may send one client's `session` cookie to other clients.
urllib3 before 1.26.5 has an issue where an application could raise a TypeError if a server responds with an unexpected error payload.
Requests allows leaking Proxy-Authorization headers to destination servers when redirected to an HTTPS endpoint.
lodash versions prior to 4.17.21 are vulnerable to Command Injection via the template function.
React contains a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability when user-supplied input is not properly sanitized before being rendered.
